description:
An automation specialist has asked you to record how long the existing workflow
  script takes to run, so that the data can be fed into a larger CI/CD dashboard
  later on.

  1. The script that must be benchmarked already exists at:
        /home/user/scripts/workflow.sh
     (It is executable; you do **not** have to create or modify it.)

  2. Measure the script’s wall-clock, user-CPU and system-CPU time with **GNU time**
     (the `/usr/bin/time` binary, not the Bash builtin).  
     • Format string **must** be:
         real=%e user=%U sys=%S  
       where  
         %e → elapsed “real” time in seconds,  
         %U → user CPU seconds,  
         %S → system CPU seconds.  
     • The three numeric fields **must be rendered with exactly two digits after
       the decimal point** (e.g. `real=0.04 user=0.00 sys=0.01`).

  3. Create the directory `/home/user/perf_reports` if it does not yet exist.

  4. Write the timing information to the file
        /home/user/perf_reports/workflow_time.log
     Overwrite the file if it already exists.

  5. The final log file **must contain exactly one line, no trailing blank
     lines or extra text**, matching this template (literal spacing required):

        real=<nnn.nn> user=<nnn.nn> sys=<nnn.nn>

     Example (numbers will differ):

        real=0.05 user=0.00 sys=0.01

  A single well-constructed terminal command can satisfy all requirements.
  Once the command finishes, no further action is needed.
truth:
Initial filesystem state created for the test runner:

  1. Directory: /home/user/scripts  (permissions 755)

  2. File: /home/user/scripts/workflow.sh  (permissions 755)
     Exact contents:
       #!/usr/bin/env bash
       # Simulated workflow
       sleep 0.05
       echo "Workflow step completed"

  3. Directory /home/user/perf_reports does NOT exist at start.

  Post-task verification done by the test harness:

  • Confirm that /home/user/perf_reports/workflow_time.log exists.
  • Confirm that it contains exactly one non-empty line.
  • Line must match the regular expression:
        ^real=[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2} user=[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2} sys=[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}$
    (The specific numeric values are not fixed, only the format.)
  • Confirm that no extra characters or blank lines are present.
